User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the LoanZen application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

subscriptionStatus.description: The current subscription status of the user (e.g., free, one-time purchase, pro).

Loan.title: Loan
Loan.description: Represents a loan associated with a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Loan entity.

amount.description: The original amount of the loan.

interestRate.description: The interest rate of the loan.

disbursementDate.description: The date the loan was disbursed.

loanType.description: The type of loan (e.g., fixed, floating).

Payment.title: Payment
Payment.description: Represents a payment made on a loan.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Payment entity.

paymentDate.description: The date the payment was made.

RateHistory.title: RateHistory
RateHistory.description: Represents a change in the interest rate for a floating rate loan.

id.description: Unique identifier for the RateHistory entity.

effectiveDate.description: The date the new rate became effective.

definition.description: Stores user profiles. Includes the 'id', 'email', and 'subscriptionStatus' fields.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user, corresponding to the Firebase Auth UID.

definition.description: Stores loan details associated with a specific user. Includes denormalized 'subscriptionStatus' from the user document for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user owning the loan.

params.name: loanId
params.description: The unique identifier for the loan.

definition.description: Stores payment details for a specific loan owned by a user. Includes denormalized 'subscriptionStatus' from the user document and 'loanType' from the loan document for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user owning the loan and payment.

params.name: loanId
params.description: The unique identifier for the loan associated with the payment.

params.name: paymentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the payment.

definition.description: Stores the rate history for a specific loan owned by a user. Includes denormalized 'subscriptionStatus' from the user document and 'loanType' from the loan document for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user owning the loan and rate history.

params.name: loanId
params.description: The unique identifier for the loan associated with the rate history.

params.name: rateId
params.description: The unique identifier for the rate history entry.

firestore.reasoning: The Firestore structure is designed to ensure authorization independence, clarity, and scalability for the LoanZen application.  User data and their associated loans, payments, and rate histories are organized in a hierarchical structure under the `/users/{userId}` path. This enables simple, path-based security rules for user-owned data. Crucially, to achieve authorization independence, any authorization-relevant data from the user document (e.g., `subscriptionStatus`) that is needed to control access to loans, payments, or rate histories MUST be copied into these subcollections.  This denormalization avoids the need for `get()` calls in security rules, enabling atomic operations. For example, if a `pro` subscription is required to access detailed loan information, the `subscriptionStatus` should be copied into each `loan` document. This design supports QAPs (Rules are not Filters) because listing operations are always scoped to a user's data. Since the data is segregated by user ID, rules can efficiently enforce that a user can only access data under their own `/users/{userId}` path.
